response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web software engineer with nearly two decades of experience, but his background is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires SoC and IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture knowledge, and deep-learning workload optimization at a principal level — none of which appear in his history. His closest relevant experience is low-latency backend work in DLang and a C/C++ stint at National Instruments, which falls well short of the hardware performance architecture expertise required.
strengths: Deep, broad software engineering experience (~18 years) across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Genuine low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering culture: TDD, open-source authorship, teaching, and community contributions
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning training/inference, GPU, or AI-accelerator workload experience
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture at principal hardware level

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware co-design background
No AI accelerator, GPU, or deep-learning workload experience
Solid low-latency and systems programming fundamentals
